name: Student Veteran Center
summary: UT Austin SVC is a lounge and meeting space where student veterans can study, socialize and relax between classes. Equipped with Wi-Fi access, a public computer workstation and a flat screen television, the center offers events and programs for veterans.

name: Actuarial Science Club
summary: Enhance opportunities for social interaction for students who are interested in actuarial science. All members gain experience and knowledge of the actuary field, its past, and its future.

name: The Alexander Hamilton Society at UT Austin
summary: The Alexander Hamilton Society at UT Austin is the local chapter of an independent, non-partisan, not-for-profit membership organization that promotes constructive debate on contemporary issues in foreign, economic, and national security policy

name: Duren Residence Hall Council
summary: Our purpose as a council is to represent the residents of Duren Hall in all their needs by sponsoring a wide array of social, academic, self-interest, and self-awareness activities.

name: Beta Beta Beta Biological Honor Society
summary: The purpose of the BBB Biological Honor Society shall be to function as an honor and professional society for students of the biological sciences. We host service, academic and social events and are open to any Undergraduate student interested in Biology

name: Dean's Scholars Student Association
summary: To support students in the Dean's Scholars Honors Program and provide them with the best undergraduate experience, while encouraging scientific engagement and undergraduate research across the university.

name: Chi Phi Fraternity
summary: The Chi Phi Fraternity is a social fraternity at The University of Texas that is committed to cultivating the next generation of leaders through academic success, community engagement, and personal growth.

name: Chicano/Hispanic/Latino Law Students' Association
summary: Foster communication of a bicultural nature in an effort to bring understanding of legal problems related to the Chicano/Hispanic community; provide common base of understanding for the Chicano/ Hispanic law students and provide them with support in ov...

name: Graduate Organization for Comparative Literature Students
summary: The mission of GRACLS (the Graduate Organization for Comparative Literature Students) is to foster a collegial and supportive Comparative Literature community that embraces the diversity of our scholarship.

name: Hispanic Business Student Association
summary: HBSA is dedicated to promoting professionalism, academics, community service, and family. Our mission is to provide a medium to enhance ourselves academically and culturally & serve as an information center for scholastic and occupational opportunity

name: International Affairs Society
summary: The International Affairs Society (IAS) is a professional and social organization that caters to students with an interest in international relations, global affairs, public service, and cultural interaction.

name: Longhorn Energy Club
summary: The social hub for all things energy at UT Austin. Open to both undergraduate and graduate students from all disciplines.

name: Alpha Epsilon Delta - Pre-Health Honor Society
summary: Alpha Epsilon Delta is the nation's oldest and largest pre-health honor society. The Texas Alpha chapter of the University of Texas at Austin serves to prepare undergraduate students for future professional school aspirations.

name: Bilingual Education Student Organization
summary: The purpose of B.E.S.O. (Bilingual Education Student Organization) is to promote the content, the procedure, and the philosophy of bilingual-bicultural education at the University of Texas at Austin.

name: Black Business Student Association
summary: The Black Business Student Association is a professional organization established to serve Black and business-minded students. BBSA aims to unite our members under the common goal of serving the African-American community through various initiatives.

name: Black Health Professions Organization- MAPS Chapter
summary: Uplifting future health professionals and engaging with the community. Meetings every other Wednesday!

name: Delta Sigma Phi Fraternity
summary: Delta Sigma Phi Fraternity is a non-hazing organization devoted to the cultivation of a cohesive brotherhood organized around ideals designed to promote leadership, scholarship, and loyalty.

name: Delta Sigma Pi
summary: Delta Sigma Pi is a professional fraternity organized to foster the study of business in universities; to encourage scholarship, social activity and the association of students for their mutual advancement by research and practice; to promote closer af..

name: Queer Business Student Association
summary: The Queer Business Student Association serves to curate an open, professional environment; promoting workplace equality for UT Austin students.

name: Graduate Engineering Council
summary: The primary purpose of the GEC is to represent the interests of the graduate engineers at UT Austin. The GEC fulfills this purpose, serving as a liaison between the Office of the Dean of Engineering.

name: Graduate Student Art History Association
summary: The objectives of GSAHA are to encourage and develop relationships between M.A. and Ph.D. candidates, faculty, visiting scholars, alumni, and other professionals in the fields; to provide a forum through which graduates students may address issues.

name: Iranian Students Academic and Cultural Organization
summary: ISACO is a student cultural organization at the University of Texas at Austin dedicated to developing and nurturing the Austin Persian community.

name: The Longhorn Rocketry Association
summary: The Longhorn Rocketry Association is a student-run organization which designs, builds, and tests high-power rockets. We aim to provide hands-on experience with industry-standard design processes, fabrication techniques, and testing and launch procedures.

name: Alpha Phi
summary: Founded in 1920, the Omega Chapter at The University of Texas at Austin was the first Alpha Phi chapter in Texas. Our members share a commitment to excellence and a strong desire to help one another and be of service to our community.

name: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc.
summary: Our purpose is to promote scholarship, brotherhood, fellowship and love for all mankind in a unified effort and therein render service to our community....

name: American Marketing Association, UT Collegiate Chapter
summary: Texas American Marketing Association strives to provide its members with the resources and opportunities to learn how to market themselves “remarkably."

name: American Society of Civil Engineers
summary: Since 1921, the American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE) at the University of Texas has been a perfect compliment to and integral part of the educational experience and career preparation for civil engineering students here at the University.

name: Engineers for a Sustainable World
summary:
ESW mobilizes students through education, technical projects, and collaborative action to impact local and global sustainability challenges.

For updated information, go here:

http://www.eswut.com/
Instagram @ESWUT

name: Graduate Student Executive Committee
summary: To represent the interests of the graduate students in the department of geological sciences.

name: Habitat for Humanity Campus Chapter
summary: Our organization helps provide affordable housing for low-income families while raising awareness of the lack of affordable housing in Central Texas. Our members love to attend builds, work alongside future homeowners, and fundraise for this mission.

name: Jester East Residence Hall Council
summary: Jester East RHC was established to promote community, embrace diversity, and foster positive experiences for the residents of Jester East through interactive and stimulating social and cultural programs and events.

name: Kappa Delta
summary: Here at Sigma Epsilon, we pride ourselves in sisterhood, philanthropy, leadership, and academics. We are currently recognized by Kappa Delta Nationals as a Merit Chapter, which means that we are in the top 10% of all 162 Kappa Delta chapters nationally.

name: American Association of Drilling Engineers
summary: The dissemination of knowledge of the theory and practice of all aspects of drilling engineering and the furtherance of the professional development of the student member....

name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics
summary: To further, within its locality, the objectives and programs of the American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics, Inc., i.e., advancing the arts, sciences, and technology of aeronautics, astronautics and hydronautics....

name: American Institute of Chemical Engineers
summary: Expose Chemical Engineering Students to academic and career opportunities, while also providing a fun social environment for peer networking.

name: Campus Events + Entertainment
summary: To plan programs that educate, develop, entertain, and enlighten the students, faculty, and staff of The University of Texas at Austin and the surrounding community.

name: EERI (Earthquake Engineering Research Institute)
summary: Our goal is to expose students to state-of-the-art earthquake engineering research and design practices, as well as to provide an additional means of communicating UT research findings to other students and to the industry.

name: Marine Science Institute Graduate Student Association
summary: To support and to be of service to all graduate students in residence at the Marine Science Institute at Port Aransas through many varied programs and projects.

name: Mechanical Engineering Graduate Student Board
summary: The Mechanical Engineering Graduate Student Board acts as a liaison between ME graduate students and faculty. MEGSB also aids in professional development of graduate students and seeks to enhance the graduate student experience.

name: Architectural Engineering Institute
summary: The Architectural Engineering Institute at UT is a nationally affiliated student organization devoted to providing architectural and civil engineering students with a link of communication to companies.

name: Central Texas Model United Nations
summary: Central Texas Model UN competes in and hosts Model United Nations Conferences. CTMUN hosts two conferences for high/middle school students, sponsors a traveling MUN team, and fosters a sense of community for all UT students engaged in global affairs.

name: Arnold Air Society John H. Payne Squadron
summary: The organization is aimed at furthering the development of Air Force officers by creating a closer and more efficient relationship within the Air Force officer training program through means of service projects, fundraisers, and social gatherings.

name: Asian Business Students Association
summary: The Asian Business Students Association serves to develop our members into multidimensional leaders in the business world.

name: Asian Pacific American Law Student Association
summary: The Asian Pacific American Law Student Association (APALSA) is an academic, professional, and social organization dedicated to promoting the interests, and addressing the concerns, of the Asian Pacific Islander (API) community.

name: Hong Kong Students Association
summary: Hong Kong Student Association at the University of Texas at Austin serves the Cantonese-speaking students community as a social group.

name: Honors Business Association
summary: To promote interaction between honor students as a group, provide a forum for discussion about the honors program and the college in general and represent the honor students in all appropriate functions....

name: Phi Kappa Sigma
summary: The Objects of Phi Kappa Sigma fraternity shall be the promotion of good fellowship and the cultivation of the social virtues among our members; the protection of just rights and the advancement of the best interests, present and future, individual and...

name: Public Interest Law Association
summary: Promote public interest law at the University of Texas School of Law.

name: College Republicans at Texas
summary: To proactively advance principles and values of the Republican Party on campus, to coordinate and provide logistical support to the Republican Party of Texas, and to engage and educate the larger student population on contemporary political issues.

name: Communication Council
summary: Our council's main purpose is to address student concerns in the College of Communication by fostering and maintaining a strong relationship with our college's administration. Additionally, we facilitate interactions between students, faculty , and sta...

name: Foot in the Door Theatre
summary: Foot in the Door is a student-run theatre group at UT Austin. We put on 1-3 shows a year, with students involved/leading all aspects of the productions. All students are welcome to audition!

name: French and Italian Graduate Student Organization
summary: We are a non-profit educational group dedicated to the representation and support for the academic, social and professional growth of graduate students in the department of French and Italian.

name: IEEE Power and Energy Society
summary:
IEEE-PES provides networking opportunities and technical project involvement for students interested in power and energy.

http://sites.utexas.edu/ieeepes/

name: IEEE Robotics and Automation Society
summary: A student org focused on robotics! We accept all majors and all levels of robotics experience. Check out our website at ras.ece.utexas.edu and https://linktr.ee/UT.RAS (our linktree) for updates!

name: Indian Cultural Association
summary: With various events and meetings, our ultimate mission is to foster a strong appreciation for diversity and the preservation of the Indian culture on the campus and in the community as a whole.

name: Liberal Arts Council
summary: Liberal Arts Council serves as the official voice of the students in the College of Liberal Arts. We are no longer accepting applications to join the Liberal Arts Council for Spring 2024. We look forward to seeing any potential applicants apply Fall 2024

name: Liberal Arts Honors Student Council
summary: We plan social events, and provide academic enrichment and service opportunities to engage the LAH community.

name: National Student Speech, Language and Hearing Association
summary: UT Austin chapter of National Student Speech Language Hearing Association (NSSLHA)

name: Natural Sciences Council
summary: Natural Sciences Council was founded in 1972 to serve as a liaison between the students and the faculty in the College of Natural Sciences (CNS). We work with the college and its students to create resources and improve the CNS student experience.

name: Senate of College Councils
summary: To serve as the official voice of students in academic affairs through representation by the College Councils.

name: Social Work Council
summary: Social Work Council is intended to advocate for social work students within the Steve Hicks School of Social Work (SHSSW), within the broader UT community, and in the state of Texas.

name: American Library Association & Society of American Archivists UT Student Chapter
summary: To serve as a means of introducing and integrating new librarians and archivists into the profession.

name: Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers
summary: Our purpose is to prepare Asian heritage scientists and engineers for success in the global business world, to celebrate diversity and participate socially on campus, and to provide opportunities for members to make contributions to their local community

name: Thai Association of Austin
summary: Thai Association of Austin was founded since 1973 with the mission to foster the Thai community and Thai culture in Austin. We organize social and cultural events for our members and provide any needs for new members.

name: Theta Tau - Professional Engineering Fraternity
summary: The Purpose of Theta Tau is to develop and maintain a high standard of professional interest among its members, and to unite them in a strong bond of fraternal fellowship. Come check out our website at https://texasthetatau.org!

name: Thurgood Marshall Legal Society
summary: The Thurgood Marshall Legal Society exists to support Black law students in their academic, social, and professional endeavors.

name: Turing Scholars Student Association
summary: The Turing Scholars Student Association is an association for students in the Turing Scholars Honors Program of the Computer Science Department.

name: Undergraduate Business Council
summary: By serving as the umbrella organization for the McCombs School of Business, the Undergraduate Business Council's mission is to help organize and unite the McCombs Affiliated Student Organizations to make a strong student body that supports its school.

name: University Democrats
summary: To increase campus civic engagement, support local canidates, and to fight for the progressive reforms needed to build a more fair just and equitable world - and to have a blast while doing it. Meetings are Wednesdays at 8PM

name: University Fashion Group
summary: Founded in 1978, University Fashion Group (UFG) is UT Austin’s premier professional fashion organization, helping students of all majors explore the industry and gain real-world experience through NYFW participation, our Capstone Fashion Show, and more.

name: University Management & Business Research Association
summary: The University Management and Business Research Association is a pro bono consulting organization that uses design thinking to empower students to create real-world solutions for both large and local business clients.

name: Amateur Radio Club at the University of Texas at Austin
summary: UTARC/N5XU is the official Amateur Radio club of the University of Texas at Austin. We are students, faculty and staff who enjoy amateur radio operations with all modes on the main ham radio bands. Our club welcomes everyone and joining is 100% free.

name: Texas Leadership Education and Development
summary: Texas LEAD in the Office of the Dean of Students is an office that supports every student at UT through leadership education and development and works to ensure that each student has the potential to create positive change.

name: University Securities Investment Team
summary: USIT is the premier organization for investing. Our members manage a ~$60,000 real-money portfolio. All grades and majors are welcome to join.

name: University Theatre Guild
summary: University Theatre Guild serves as a creative outlet for theatre lovers of all experience levels who want to surround themselves with like-minded individuals. UTG is completely autonomous in its funding, management, and production.

name: TX Votes
summary: TX Votes is a nonpartisan student organization dedicated to increasing civic engagement across UT's campus through voter registration, tabling, partnering with other student orgs, hosting fun civic events, and so much more!

name: Women in Business Association
summary: Women in Business Association (WBA) strives to increase our members' knowledge of and involvement in the world of business by inviting educated and experienced professionals to speak and share advice with our organization.

name: Women in Computer Science
summary: To provide a community for Women in Computer Science to meet other women, network with top technology companies, and encourage more women to join the technology field.

name: Women in Psychology
summary: We are built on the principles of career exploration in psychology and related fields, connection with motivated individuals, and discovery of UT resources. All degrees and genders are welcome, with ongoing membership enrollment throughout the semester.

name: Women's Law Caucus
summary: Women’s Law Caucus seeks to propel women into successful roles as both students at Texas Law and as future lawyers. Our mission is to create an environment of support for women in the law school and enhance the role of women in the legal profession.

name: Zeta Tau Alpha
summary: The object of this organization shall be to promote the purpose and programs of the fraternity; to function as a constructive, vital force in the lives of its members; to cooperate with the local alumnae; and to serve as a cooperative, active part of t..

name: Austin Chapter of SIAM
summary: To promote interaction between members of the applied mathematics community at UT Austin, across departments, institutes, and professional marks. To provide a forum for the discussion of applied and computational mathematics and pedagogy; To help membe..

name: Longhorn Athletic Training Student Association
summary: Student organization which aims to strengthen and advance the profession of athletic training by promoting education, professionalism, leadership, and volunteerism.

name: Omega Chi Epsilon - Chemical Engineering
summary: The objective of Omega Chi Epsilon (OXE) is to recognize and promote high scholarship, original investigation, camaraderie, and professional service in the McKetta Department of Chemical Engineering at the University of Texas at Austin.

name: Plan II Students Association
summary: Promote student community within Plan II

name: OUTLaw - LGBTQ Law Student Association
summary: OUTLaw seeks to unify and promote the interests of queer law students.

name: Texas Phi Alpha Delta
summary: Texas Phi Alpha Delta works to develop informed and competitive pre-law students through fostering a community that is supportive, engaging, and beneficial for all members, both during their undergraduate careers and after.

name: UT Chapter Omicron Delta Epsilon- Economics Honor Society
summary: ODE@UT strives to confer distinction for scholastic achievement in economics; promote interest in all aspects of economics; support undergraduate journal "The Developing Economist"; and provide mentorship for members.

name: Texas Orange Jackets
summary: Texas Orange Jackets is one of UT's oldest honorary service and spirit organization that uplifts nonbinary, transgender, gender-diverse, and female-identitying communities who seek to better The University and the City of Austin, to empower everyone to b

name: Society of Physics Students
summary: We are a student-led organization open to anyone with a love of physics. We feature year-round guest lectures & talks from professors and others in the industry, information sessions, socials, and competitions.

name: Student Bar Association
summary: The student body of the law school is organized as a Student Bar Association, the membership of which includes all students in the school. The Board of Governors is in charge of the association and acts as the student government of the law school.

name: Supply Chain Management Student Organization
summary: An organization for students studying or just interested in Supply Chain, Logistics, and Operations to connect with fellow peers and career opportunities. Come to our Wednesday meetings to meet new friends with similar interests!

name: Tau Beta Pi - Engineering
summary: Tau Beta Pi is an interdisciplinary engineering honor society that recognizes students with distinguished scholarship and exemplary character. Established in 1885, TBP is the second-oldest collegiate honor society.

name: Texas Advertising Group
summary: TAG's missions is to help students stand out by providing them with a network of peers and professionals who will help them pave a path to a successful career in advertising.

name: Texas Belles Event Planning Organization
summary: Texas Belles is a pre-professional event planning organization for students interested in the hospitality industry. We are affiliated with the Moody College of Communication but do not restrict membership to those majors.

name: Texas Economics Association
summary: The purpose of the Texas Economics Association, as the oldest non-national economics student association at the University of Texas at Austin, is to provide professional, academic, social and philanthropic opportunities to its members.

name: Texas Intellectual Property Law Journal
summary: Our primary focus centers on providing information on significant and innovative contributions to U.S. and international intellectual property law by publishing a regular journal.

name: Texas International Law Journal
summary: The Texas International Law Journal (TILJ) is a student-run organization that delivers leading scholarship related to international legal, theoretical, and policy analysis.

name: Texas Journal of Oil, Gas & Energy Law
summary: The Texas Journal of Oil, Gas, and Energy Law's (TJOGEL) vision is to be a leading energy law journal. We want to be the forum of choice for energy law practitioners, professors, and students around the globe. Our focus centers on providing significant..

name: Texas Law Fellowships
summary: Texas Law Fellowships (TLF) is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization that sponsors public interest fellowships for students at the University of Texas School of Law.

name: Texas Review of Law and Politics
summary: The Unfettered Pursuit of Conservative Legal Scholarship

name: Texas Royals
summary: Texas Royals is a spirit organization for women and non-binary students on campus. Our mission is to enhance the college experience of our members through involvement in philanthropic, spirit, and social  activities, along with leadership opportunities.

name: Texas Stock Team
summary: Texas Stock Team (TST) is an organization of The University of Texas at Austin that was created during Spring 2013 semester. TST teaches members financial concepts in valuation methodologies, accounting, and corporate finance.

name: Texas Student Psychological Association
summary: Our mission is to provide students with opportunities to explore the world of psychology through guest speakers, volunteer projects, workshops, and socials.

name: Texas Theme Park Engineering and Design
summary: Our purpose is to expose engineering students to the industry of Theme Park and Amusement entertainment by conducting hands on activities that allow them to have design experience. We also hope to expose them to other TPED groups in the country.

name: Longhorn Legislative Aide Agency of Student Government
summary: LLA is an internship within Student Government that works with the Executive Board and Assembly in order to deal with issues on campus. Mentors, Friends, and memories come with being an LLA. LLA will mature each intern in the professional world.

name: Office of the Dean of Students
summary: The Office of the Dean of Students provides opportunities and experiences that support student learning and personal development.

name: Chemical Engineering Graduate Leadership Council
summary: This council is responsible for the management of professional enrichment, social, financial, and recruitment activities as it pertains to chemical engineering graduate students at UT.

name: Diversity in Advertising & PR
summary: DAPR is a student-run organization that focuses and celebrates diversity in the Ad/PR sphere to empower underrepresented talent. DAPR was created to connect members of all backgrounds with professionals, strengthen portfolios, and build community at UT.

name: Neuroscience Graduate Students' Association
summary: NGSA is the Interdisciplinary Neuroscience Program's graduate student organization. We organize social and outreach events for INP students and bring together a community for all who are interested in neuroscience

name: Sikh Students Association
summary: To promote the values and to spread awareness of Sikhi through events involving the university community and the Austin community.

name: Election Supervisory Board
summary: The Election Supervisory Board (ESB) is tasked with conducting and overseeing all campus-wide student elections.

name: Omega Phi Gamma Fraternity
summary: Omega Phi Gamma is an Asian-interest fraternity established in the Spring of 1995 at the University of Texas at Austin. We strive to promote brotherhood, leadership, academics and service within the Asian-American community.

name: Student Emergency Services
summary: Student Emergency Services, in the Office of the Dean of Students, works with campus and community organizations to provide a number of services to assist students, and in some cases their families and friends, during an emergency or crisis situation.

name: University Unions
summary: Representing five centers for student life, the University Unions is an organization committed to enhancing the campus community through a variety of facilities (HMA, SAC, SSB, TXUN), programs and services (E+E).

name: Texas Student Media
summary: TSM is comprised of several student-produced media properties: The Daily Texan newspaper; the humor magazine Texas Travesty; the Cactus yearbook; KVRX 91.7 FM; Texas Student Television (TSTV), and BurntX.

name: Student Governance Organizations
summary: Student Governance Organizations is an area within The Office of the Dean of Students.  We assist with administrative, financial and programatic support of Student Government and the Senate of College Councils.

name: Texas Global
summary: In an increasingly interconnected world, international education is more important than ever, and UT is committed to educating global citizens and carrying out research that changes lives and addresses the world’s greatest challenges.

name: The Center for Students in Recovery
summary: The Center for Students in Recovery (CSR) supports students recovering from addictions at the University of Texas at Austin.

name: Longhorn Scholars Program
summary: Longhorn Scholars Program is a four-year program that serves top students from selected Texas high schools whose graduates have historically been underrepresented at The University of Texas at Austin.

name: Longhorn Orientation
summary: Longhorn Orientation welcomes thousands of incoming students each year, ensuring their successful transition, both academically and socially, to the University.

name: Opportunities for Undergraduate Research at UT Austin
summary: OUR UT is an undergraduate research program for incoming freshmen students. Students take part in higher education research projects working with a student mentor, graduate students, faculty and student affairs professionals.

name: Opportunities for Undergraduate Research at UT Austin Student Mentors
summary: The OUR UT Student Mentors are former students in the OUR UT program. They act as mentors to the incoming freshmen who participate in OUR UT. They also take part in continuing research out of the DoS Research Institute.

name: Jackson School of Geosciences
summary: The JSG includes the University’s Department of Geological Sciences - one of the country's oldest geoscience departments - and two world-renowned research units -- the Institute for Geophysics and the Bureau of Economic Geology.

name: Center for Asian American Studies
summary: The Center for Asian American Studies (CAAS) at The University of Texas at Austin is an interdisciplinary academic program promoting understanding and awareness of Asian Pacific American (APA) issues and communities.

name: DoS Graduate Assistant Supervisors
summary: A group of professional staff members who supervise graduate assistants.

name: ATX Science Olympiad
summary: We are a STEM outreach organization that runs Science Olympiad competitions for 6th - 12th graders and seeks to aid in the establishment of SciOly teams in middle and high schools across Texas, increasing the accessibility of team-based STEM learning.

name: Longhorn Link Program
summary: The Longhorn Link Program provides students with learning support, leadership development, and enrichment opportunities to ensure academic excellence and graduation.

name: UTeach-Liberal Arts
summary: UTeach-Liberal Arts is a professional teacher preparation program for undergraduate and post-baccalaureate students planning to teach middle school or high school English, Languages Other Than English, or social studies.

name: Longhorn Center for Academic Excellence
summary: Longhorn Center for Academic Excellence creates avenues facilitating the capacity of all students to take charge of their learning and their lives.

name: Undergraduate Computational Finance Team
summary: Texas Undergraduate Computational Finance is a student run trading organization that educates UT undergraduates about quantitative methods in finance.

name: Polymathic Scholars Student Leadership Panel
summary: The Polymathic Scholars Student Leadership Panel is the student organization of the Polymathic Scholars Honors Program in the College of Natural Sciences.

name: Dream Corps at the University of Texas at Austin
summary: Dream Corps at UT works closely with students, educators, and communities to raise awareness for children in rural China. Indicated by its slogan “Let Reading Inspire”, Dream Corps at UT is dedicated to promoting reading among children.

name: Texas Bluebonnets
summary: The purpose of the Texas Bluebonnets is to unite diverse UT students through service, social, and spirit events. The Texas Bluebonnets' main focus is on leadership and character development.

name: Texas Table Top
summary:
To provide a variety of tabletop/board games for students to learn and enjoy.  
We have many games both popular and niche for our members to enjoy and play!

name: Student Academy of Audiology chapter at The University of Texas at Austin
summary: To introduce students to lifelong involvement in activities that promote and advance the profession of audiology, and provides services, information, education, representation and advocacy for the profession and the public we serve.

name: Information and Systems Security Society (ISSS)
summary: ISSS teaches students about security and privacy through talks, workshops, and Capture the Flag competitions (CTFs). More information can be found on our website, isss.io.

name: United Mission for Relief & Development Texas Chapter
summary: United Mission for Relief & Development - Austin aims to provide relief to those in the local community with a focus on building the future leaders of our community while improving ourselves in the service of others.

name: Society for Advancement of Chicano/Hispanics and Native Americans in Science
summary: Our group is intended to encourage the success of Chicano/Hispanic and Native American students in all stages of their scientific careers. Our goal is to make practicing science at any level more equitable and accessible for underrepresented students.

name: Women of Excellence
summary: Women of Excellence connects well-established UT leaders with incoming first and second-year women and nonbinary individuals. Texas WOE was founded on six pillars: mentorship, leadership, empowerment, service, diversity, and professionalism.

name: American Society for Microbiology at The University of Texas at Austin
summary: The American Society for Microbiology at UT Austin connects our members with the brilliant and diverse field of microbiology—the study of how microorganisms shape ecology, medicine, and industry.

name: Horns for Life
summary: We are a consistent life organization working to spread a culture of life on campus through outreach, volunteering, and awareness.

name: Council of Graduate Chemists
summary: The Council of Graduate Chemists serves on behalf of graduate students in the Department of Chemistry by means of fostering professional and community relationships, informing about school-wide policies, and advocating for our peers needs.

name: Academic Counselors Association
summary: Staff organization. Our goal is to build recognition of the importance of effective academic advising and enhance the professional development of advisors.

name: Radio-Television-Film Department
summary: One of the top most desired majors at UT, RTF is one of a handful of film programs in the country that's ranked in the top ten in both film production and in the academic study of media.

name: College of Education Career Service
summary: Career Services at the College of Education

name: SPARK Magazine
summary: Student-run multimedia publication pushing the boundaries of art, culture, and fashion. | www.sparkmagazinetx.com

name: IFORTY Administrative Professional Network
summary: A network that supports and promotes innovative administrative professionals on the University of Texas at Austin campus who demonstrate the power of the individual in providing daily support to the mission of the University and the Forty Acres.

name: Association of Professionals in Student Affairs
summary: APSA focuses on Student Affairs Professionals at The University of Texas at Austin and creating an environment where information, best practices, current trends and resources are shared with colleagues across campus.

name: Longhorn Quiz Bowl
summary: Longhorn Quiz Bowl competes in academic tournaments locally and across the nation. In the 2021-2022 season, Longhorn Quiz Bowl came third nationally at the Intercollegiate Championship Tournament. Both undergraduate and graduate students are welcome.

name: Safety Education Program
summary: In order to continue to create a safer UT Community, all registered & sponsored student organizations complete the Safety Education Program annually. Safety Education connects student organizations to risk management, prevention, and emergency resources.

name: Texas Tai Chi
summary: no longer active

name: UNIVERSITY POLICE ORGANIZATION
summary: To Promote the professionalism and training of UTPD Staff. To promote police and public trust and interaction.

name: Pride and Equity Faculty Staff Association
summary: PEFSA is a University Resource Group established in the Fall of 2006 to promote the interests of LGBTQ and allied faculty and staff at UT Austin. All UT faculty and staff are welcome to join. Help us to make a difference!

name: Texas Real Estate Law Society
summary: The mission of the Texas Real Estate Law Society is to provide networking, educational, professional development, and leadership opportunities for law students interested in Real Estate and related areas of the law.

name: International Business Student Association
summary: The purpose of International Business Student Association is to expand member’s knowledge of global issues that affect the way we do business. We are committed to international and cultural awareness, professional and social development, and leadership.

name: Structural Engineering Association of Texas - Student Chapter
summary: The Structural Engineers Association of Texas (SEAoT) Student Chapter at The University of Texas at Austin seeks to enhance students’ understanding of the structural engineering field through technical discussions with professionals and professors.

name: Lang Stuttering Institute Student Leadership Organization
summary: The Lang Stuttering Institute Student Leadership Organization seeks to unite university students from diverse backgrounds and majors in order to educate, advocate, and spread awareness about stuttering.

name: Alternative Breaks
summary: Alternative Breaks (AB) sends small groups of UT students to outside communities to grow as leaders and learn through immersive service experiences.

name: INFORMS Student Chapter at The University of Texas at Austin
summary: Encourage interest in Operations Research (OR) and the Management Sciences (MS); provide an informal forum for people interested in the educational programs, opportunities, methods, and techniques of ORMS; sponsor colloquia whose speakers address ORMS.

name: Freetail Hackers
summary: An org focused on fostering a community of students who like to build, program, design, and experiment with technology regardless of major.

name: Machine Learning and Data Science
summary: We host workshops and events on topics in machine learning and data science - students of all levels and majors are welcome!

name: Women in Civil, Architectural, and Environmental Engineering
summary: WCAE is dedicated to creating an inclusive community for undergraduate women, supporting women with mentorship throughout their studies, and engaging alumni and faculty role models to advance leadership and career development in engineering.

name: Science Under the Stars
summary: Science Under the Stars is a graduate student run public lecture series. Events start at dusk outdoors at Brackenridge Field Laboratory monthly. Please only request to join if you are an Integrative Biology associated graduate student.

name: Texas DnD
summary: Texas DnD is a tabletop gaming club open to all levels of experience. Our groups generally play TTRPGs such as DnD and Pathfinder; we provide a community for like-minded individuals interested in Tabletop Role-Play Games and meeting new people.

name: Asian/Asian American Faculty and Staff Association
summary: The Asian/Asian American Faculty and Staff Association supports, celebrates, and empowers Asian and Asian American faculty and staff by fostering a community that provides opportunities for networking, communication, and personal & professional growth.

name: Apricity Magazine
summary: Apricity Magazine (www.apricitymagazine.com) is UT Austin's official literary and arts magazine. Founded in 2016 by Margaret Siu, we accept all forms of art and feature these pieces online and in a print edition every spring.

name: The Law Veterans Association at the University of Texas School of Law
summary: The Law Veterans Association at the University of Texas School of Law is composed of all current and former military and military-affiliated members of the Texas Law School community.

name: Student Chapter of the Society of Petrophysicists and Well Log Analysts at The University of Texas at Austin
summary: The Student Chapter of SPWLA at UT Austin is dedicated to advancing the science of petrophysics and formation evaluation among students and young professionals at The University of Texas at Austin.

name: Texas Convergent
summary: Texas Convergent was established to promote collaboration across the University of Texas through entrepreneurship. Convergent aims to bridge the gap between the Computer Science department and students across the university.

name: Texas Undergraduate Research Journal
summary: The Texas URJ produces an annual journal that publishes research done by undergraduates at UT Austin. Additionally, the URJ hosts panels and networking events throughout the year to introduce students to broad topics in research and in research writing.

name: The Association of Black Psychologists
summary: Texas Student Circle of Association of Black Psychologists (ABPsi) mission is to promote professional development, Black Psychology and mental health awareness at The University of Texas at Austin.

name: Texas Sunshines
summary: Texas Sunshines is a spirit group for women and non-binary folks at UT Austin founded in the Spring of 2017. Our organization and members are dedicated to service, spirit, and friendship. To learn more, visit our website at https://www.texassunshines.com

name: Animators Club
summary: Interested in 2D or 3D animation? Concept designs or storyboards? Do you enjoy animated films/TV? Then this is the club for you. The Animators Club will provide resources to all students interested in pursuing animation as a career or hobby.

name: Association for Women in Mathematics - Texas Chapter
summary: The Association for Women in Mathematics - Texas Chapter aims to promote equal opportunity and the equal treatment of women and girls in the mathematical sciences. All genders are welcome.

name: TEDxUTAustin
summary: TEDxUTAustin hosts the largest TEDx conference in Austin, offering new perspectives and granting students, professors, community members, and distinguished individuals a platform to share thoughts and unite the Austin community.

name: Hispanic Association of Computer Scientists
summary: Hispanic Association of Computer Scientists (HACS) seeks to empower Hispanic students by providing opportunities to engage in leadership, networking and mentoring with peers, faculty and professionals in the area of computer science.

name: Math Club
summary: Math Club is a social and academic organization meant for anyone interested in mathematics.

name: Texas Black Lens
summary: Black Lens is THE space for Black creatives at UT. Our mission is to establish a safe, empowering, and innovative space for Black creatives on campus to explore their artistic passions and pursuits.

name: The Triple Helix at UT Austin
summary: The Texas TTH chapter strives to discuss relevant scientific and ethical issues using a research-based perspective.

name: New Wave Longhorns
summary: New Wave Longhorns is a supportive student community that provides social activities, academic growth, and professional development for students on a nontraditional path to their degree!

name: Wealth Management Student Organization
summary: The Wealth Management Student Organization brings together individuals interested in wealth management. We teach CFP (certified financial planning) concepts and support the careers of members interested in wealth management. All majors welcome

name: Texas Pre-Law Honor Society
summary: TX-PLHS works to help pre-law students pave their path to law school by inviting guest speakers, preparing for the LSAT, and hosting networking events so that students can learn about life in law school and as an attorney.

name: Afterglow ATX
summary: We are an online music publication with a mission to publish innovative work and spotlight underrepresented artists' work.

name: The Aggie Society at Texas Law
summary: The Aggie Society at Texas Law seeks to strengthen ties between students, Texas A&M/Texas Law alumni, and the Austin community.

name: Texas Institute for Discovery Education in Science
summary: The Texas Institute for Discovery Education in Science (TIDES) in the College of Natural Sciences aims to catalyze, support, and showcase innovative, evidence-based undergraduate science education.

name: Tau Sigma at The University of Texas at Austin
summary: Tau Sigma at the University of Texas at Austin is a National Honor Society designed exclusively for transfer students on the Forty Acres.

name: Switch Energy Club Longhorn Chapter
summary: We offer the opportunity to learn about policy, engineering, & business sides behind various types of energy - biofuels, oil & gas, solar, batteries, carbon capture, & many more - via biweekly meetings, events, & our annual energy case competition.

name: Disability and Neurodivergent Law Student Alliance at The University of Texas at Austin
summary: Disability and Neurodivergent Law Student Alliance at The University of Texas at Austin aims to build community and provide support for law students with disabilities or neurodivergence, have family members or close friends with disabilities, or are just

name: The Annette Strauss Institute For Civic Life
summary: The Annette Strauss Institute for Civic Life exists to cultivate informed voters and active citizens. We do so through research, education, and outreach programs focused on three key pillars: civic discovery, young people, and civil dialogue.

name: Moody College Honors Student Council
summary: We are a student led organization elected by members of the Moody College Honors Program. We create a forum for students to have input in the program's efforts. This includes but is not limited to academic, service, and social event opportunities.

name: StudentsCare at University of Texas at Austin
summary: StudentsCare is an organization that will set up a hospital buddy system between undergraduate students at UT Austin and long-term pediatric patients at Dell Children's and Parson's House. We strive to bring companionship to patients and their families

name: Texas Consulting
summary: Texas Consulting is a student-run management consulting organization focused on advising and working on pro-bono projects for Fortune 500s, global non-profits, and venture backed startups. We focus on providing our members with hands-on experiences.

name: Association of Black Computer Scientists
summary: A student-led organization that provides community, mentorship, networking, and opportunities for underrepresented students seeking educational and professional success in Computer Science.

name: Texas Women in Economics
summary: Texas Women in Economics is dedicated to fostering a vibrant community that unites undergraduate women and allies within the Department of Economics.

name: Health Innovation Fellows
summary: The purpose of the Health Innovation Fellows is to promote the McCombs School of Business as a place that produces leaders with the capability to impact the health care industry through innovation and leadership.

name: Students Fighting Climate Change
summary: SFCC strives to raise awareness in our community on the climate crisis in an interdisciplinary way, and build relationships with other organizations on campus and in Austin to uplift each other and empower all of us to be better climate activists.

name: Journalism Graduate Student Council
summary: Journalism Graduate Student Council serves as a liaison between students and faculty within the School of Journalism and Media. We represent student voices and offer a space to share concerns as well as to foster connection and professional development.

name: Longhorn Investment Team
summary:
We build more than resumes. Longhorn Investment Team is redefining the way students learn about business with an integrative approach that spans beyond the typical investment team.

We welcome students from all majors and all disciplines.

name: Sustainability Investment Group
summary: The Sustainability Investment Group is the premier sustainability and finance group at UT. SIG offers students the opportunity to become leaders in social impact, environmental action, and community investment through their one of a kind curriculum.

name: Q++ (Queer Students in Computer Science)
summary: Q++ is an organization that aims to empower and provide support for LGBTQ+ students in computer science and technology through academic, professional, and social events.

name: Social Entrepreneurship Learning Lab Fellowship
summary: The SELL Fellowship is an intensive eight-week fellowship program for undergraduate students attending the University of Texas at Austin who want to build social impact-focused organizations to solve the BIG problems.

name: TX NLGJA
summary: NLGJA at UT Austin strives to improve coverage of LGBTQ issues and increase representation in newsrooms. Everyone is welcome to join, regardless of sexual orientation, gender, or major.

name: The Daily Texan
summary: The Daily Texan student paper at the University of Texas at Austin. Both in print and online, the Texan is responsible for keeping the UT community informed.

name: Texas Design, Build, Fly
summary: We are an organization that designs, builds, and flies a new RC aircraft every year! We want to teach any and all students who are interested in RC aircraft about how a RC aircraft functions and how to build a RC aircraft!

name: Texas Product Engineering Organization
summary: We are a group of students at UT Austin who are building, designing, and launching software products to solve real-world problems in our community.

name: University of Texas at Austin Police Department (UTPD)
summary: Founded in 1968, The University of Texas at Austin Police Department (UTPD) continues to uphold its mission to serve and protect.

name: Law Students for Black Lives
summary: Law Students for Black Lives is a student-run organization dedicated to using the law to challenge and eliminate racial disparities in the criminal justice system, amplify Black voices, and empower communities in the ongoing fight against racism.

name: Graduates for Underrepresented Minorities
summary: Graduates for Underrepresented Minorities (GUM) goal is to create a supportive community among underrepresented minority graduate students.

name: Hazing Prevention and Education
summary: The Hazing Prevention and Education strives to address hazing through a comprehensive lens and collaborative action.

name: Austin Sunshine Camps at The University of Texas at Austin
summary: Austin Sunshine Camps is the only free overnight camp for historically underserved youth. Members of ASC at UT will either serve as a camp counselor or as a fundraising champion for the nonprofit, growing as a leader and making an impact in Austin, TX.

name: Hook'd Magazine
summary: Hook'd Magazine is a student-led fashion and culture magazine aimed a giving budding creatives hands-on experience in niche creative and business spaces. We give our members a platform to gain exposure into related fields without needing prior experience

name: Kendra Scott Women's Entrepreneurial Leadership Institute Student Board
summary: The KS WELI Student Board represents the needs and experiences of students at UT to the Kendra Scott Women's Entrepreneurial Leadership Institute.

name: Partners in Health Engage at UT Austin
summary: Partners In Health Engage at UT Austin is a team of dedicated and motivated UT students who have a desire to aid in the fight towards healthcare equality. We believe that together, we can build a more just and equitable world. Help build the movement!

name: Longhorn Voices Book Club
summary: Reading and discussing entertaining #ownvoices books in an environment that aims to make everyone feel included and heard.

name: The E. Lee Walker Fellowship at Texas
summary: The E. Lee Walker Fellowship at Texas exists to cultivate conscientious and persistent student action in the pursuit of community engagement.

name: Admissions Welcome Center
summary: The Admissions Welcome Center serves as the front door to the university for prospective students and their families. Located on the ground floor of the Perry-Castañeda Library in the heart of campus.

name: Texas Robotics Graduate Students
summary: A student org to promote community, opportunities, and professional development for graduate students in Texas Robotics.

name: Title IX Office
summary: Provide leadership pertaining to the Title IX regulations, state laws, and University policies, as well as develop and implement best practices for prevention, training, resources, and support services at The University of Texas at Austin.

name: Because Inquiry Propels Our Curiosity
summary: Building Inclusive Leadership in Science Together!

name: Texas Undergraduate Sales Organization
summary: Texas Undergraduate Sales Organization (TUSO) seeks to build a community of students interested in professional sales and business. It strives to support students in their career goals and provide opportunities for learning and growth.

name: Texas Momentum
summary: Momentum provides semester-long internships to talented UT students at top quality startups. Momentum's student-founded companies have collectively raised over $1M.

name: CliftonStrengths for Students
summary: CliftonStrengths for Students is an educational program that uses Gallup's world-renowned CliftonStrengths assessment to measure 34 research-validated talent themes and provides resources tailored to students to develop their talents into strengths.

name: National Retail Federation Student Association at UT Austin
summary: The National Retail Federation Student Association at UT Austin aims to provide students interested in careers in the retail industry with unique educational programs, networking and scholarship opportunities, and access to the latest industry research.

name: Deaf Culture Alliance at the University of Texas at Austin
summary: DCA's mission is to bring together Deaf students, HoH students, and students with a passion for ASL/Deaf Culture. We work on projects together, have discussions about Deaf Rights and education, and work on making positive changes in the community.

name: Dinner with 16 Longhorns
summary: Dinner with 16 Longhorns is an opportunity for students to explore commonalities, learn about their peers, and meet people they may not typically encounter on campus.

name: Longhorn Harm Reduction Coalition
summary: Longhorn Harm Reduction Coalition is a student organization supported by the Impact Factory and UT SHIFT, helping students get involved in harm reduction efforts through education, service, and advocacy.

name: Texas Society Of Unconventional Drummers
summary: UT's newest musical group focused on experimenting with alternative percussion and unconventional musical techniques.

name: The Texas Business Law Society
summary: Texas Business Law Society (TBLS) is dedicated to providing the highest quality educational, networking, and professional development opportunities for law students interested in transaction work. TBLS will abide by all University rules.

name: Texas Energy Capital
summary: Texas Energy Capital is a student-managed investment organization at the University of Texas at Austin focused on providing students exposure to the energy industry through management of an energy-investment portfolio.

name: Texas Equity Group
summary: Texas Equity Group is the premier investing group at UT with the mission of fostering and advancing our members' interest and education in finance.

name: The Alzheimer's and Dementia Organization at The University of Texas at Austin
summary: We wish to increase awareness and advocacy, volunteering opportunities, and foster education for and about Alzheimer's and other dementias at the University of Texas at Austin.

name: Engineering Student Life
summary:
Engineering Student Life 
Cockrell School of Engineering

name: ADAPT Texas
summary: ADAPT Texas creates, adapts, and develops tools for individuals with disabilities, advocate for communities, and create change, one person at a time.

name: Kappa Theta Pi at the University of Texas at Austin
summary:
UT Austin's premier co-ed technology fraternity centered around five pillars: professional development, alumni connections, social growth, technological advancement, and academic support.

Open to all majors.

name: Active Minds at The University of Texas at Austin
summary: Dedicated to building stronger communities through education, research, advocacy, and a focus on young adults, Active Minds is opening up the conversation about mental health and creating lasting change in the way mental health is talked about.

name: Texas Creative Union Project
summary: TCUP is a collective organization aimed to support student artists by providing a welcoming art market community to grow and learn together.

name: Austin STEM Buddies
summary: We aim to promote STEM exploration in elementary and secondary students around the Austin area.

name: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity Incorporated
summary: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity is a professional organization of college-educated men with similar ideas and like attainments.

name: Out of State, International, Transfer Student Agency of Student Government
summary: The International, Out of State, and Transfer Student Agency (IOTSA) advocates and provides support and a community space for students from other states, international students, and students who have transferred from other universities.

name: Longhorn Federal Bar Association
summary: The Longhorn Federal Bar Association seeks to create a community for Texas Law students interested in federal government practice and aid students searching for career opportunities in federal practice and firms working with clients before the federal.

name: Plaintiffs' Advocacy & Litigation Society
summary: Provide opportunities for students to learn about plaintiff-side career paths and connect with practitioners and other students interested in plaintiffs' advocacy.

name: Undergraduate Law Journal for Minority Women
summary: A University of Texas at Austin organization uplifting and empowering women of color by helping them explore law through legal writing.

name: Undergraduate Linguistic Society
summary: The Undergraduate Linguistic Society aims to assist undergraduates at UT in becoming involved in the linguistic community through connecting students with faculty, research opportunities, and other students interested in linguistics.

name: Women in Data and Statistics
summary: Women in Data and Statistics is dedicated to providing educational resources, career opportunities, and an inclusive community for all interested in data analytics, data science, research, and statistics.

name: Longhorn Neurotech
summary: We are a neural engineering club dedicated to preparing our members for careers in academia and industry through competitions, education, and networking.

name: Youth Justice Alliance
summary: An organization to support aspiring first-generation lawyers.

name: National Association of Black Journalists - UT
summary: National Association of Black Journalists - UT is tailored toward Black students interested in Communications. We host Black professional speakers and cultivate an incredible community at our biweekly meetings. All majors are welcome!

name: Texas Entertainment Association
summary: A preprofessional student organization that seeks to educate and prepare students for a career in entertainment.

name: Collaboration for Undoing Racism in Environmental Sciences
summary: CURES is geared towards providing students with a toolbelt of techniques for fighting environmental racism either in outstanding advocacy or in their everyday lives.

name: EcoCAR at UT
summary: EcoCAR at UT modifies the latest production vehicles, integrating new software and hardware in a competition against other universities

name: Hispanic Public Relations Student Association
summary: To advance the practice of Hispanic/Latinx public relations as a communications and marketing discipline. To serve as the voice for students and rising professionals working in the communications field nationwide.

name: The Searcher
summary: UT Austin's first undergraduate humanities research journal, accepting scholarly research in the humanities for publicatoin.

name: Beyond Frames Productions
summary: Student Film Organization at The University of Texas at Austin.

name: Probate, Guardianship Law, Estates, and Trusts Society
summary: The Probate Courts process matters involving the civil and property rights of deceased and incapacitated persons. PGLETS is the primary point of contact for Texas Law students to learn, network, and volunteer in the world of probate and estate planning.

name: Texas Materials Research Society
summary: The mission of Texas Materials Research Society (MRS) is to represent and further the interests of MSE graduate students and organize department-wide events to build community within the Texas Materials Institute.

name: Texas Pre-CAA Association
summary: TPCA aims to provide prospective Certified Anesthesiologist Assistants (CAAs) students with resources, opportunities, and assistance in their pre-health journeys.

summary: Longhorn Students for Israel stands at the University of Texas at Austin, committed and dedicated to educating and uniting students through a comprehensive understanding of Israel by providing a diverse range of educational programs, speaker events

summary: Interpreters in Healthcare aims to help patients by interpreting for them primarily in Spanish in the healthcare setting. We seek to become experienced interpreters by getting our certifications through this organization and effectively helping others.

name: The International Society for Pharmaceutical Engineering
summary: The International Society for Pharmaceutical Engineering is a professional organization aimed at providing educational/career opportunities to those looking to pursue a career in pharmaceuticals or biotech.

name: Peer Assistance and Leadership Students
summary: PALs (Peer Assistance and Leadership) intends/seeks to cultivate young leaders and create a peer-to-peer mentoring program between UT Austin students and area Austin high schools.

name: Normandy Scholars Program
summary: The Frank Denius Normandy Scholar Program (NSP) studies the causes, conduct, consequences, and contemporary representations of the Second World War from multiple national perspectives.

summary: We’re a tech nonprofit cultivating the strongest cross-campus community of its kind to deliver community building, academic support, and career development opportunities at scale.

name: GIVE at The University of Texas at Austin
summary: Our goal is to create an environment where like-minded people can come together, thrive, dive into their curiosities about the world, and make a good impact. We seek to provide sustainable volunteer opportunities and collaborate with other student orgs.

name: Vertical Flight Society at UT
summary: We are an organization focused on designing and building a scale, vertical take-off aircraft to participate in the annual VFS Design, Build, Vertical Fly competition. We also host speakers and attend indsutry-specific networking events!

name: Community Engagement Collective
summary: The Community Engagement Collective is a network of students passionate about community engagement and service. Our vision is that all UT students can be active citizens of the Austin community while enrolled and can create meaningful impact.

name: Longhorn Digital Marketing
summary: Longhorn Digital Marketing is a student collective designed to uplift and educate the University of Texas community to pursue careers in the digital marketing space.

name: Swear2Care at UT
summary: Swear2Care at UT Austin supports college students' mental health through peer connections, creative expression, and advocacy, fostering a sense of belonging and reducing loneliness on campus.

name: Criminal Law Association
summary: Criminal Law Association is an organization for students to gain more knowledge on civil rights, racial equality, and the criminal justice system. If you are passionate about the legal field and want to learn more about criminal law, this org is for you!

name: The Longhorn Design Lab

name: The International Association of Business Communicators Chapter at the University of Texas at Austin
summary: The International Association of Business Communicators at the University of Texas at Austin chapter serves as a professional student-led organization dedicated to the success of the Longhorn community across sectors of the business communications field.

name: Cassidy Joined for Hope at the University of Texas
summary: Cassidy Joined for Hope at the University of Texas is a mental health and suicide awareness organization aimed at providing resources, educating, and supporting the students of UT. We promote hope, kindness, and open discussions about mental health.

name: Texas Perfect Pair
summary: We pair student volunteers with seniors at assisted living homes based on similar backgrounds, experiences, and hobbies to foster a meaningful intergenerational connection that combats loneliness and improves overall health and well-being.

name: Pharmacology and Toxicology Social and Outreach Committee
summary: The Pharmacology and Toxicology Social and Outreach Committee is group dedicated to advancing science education in local Austin schools. In addition to our educational outreach, we are known for organizing social events to foster a sense of community.

name: The New Project
summary: The New Project is a multi-media creative community founded on the bases of a new inclusive creative environment. The New Project supports and promotes its members through creative projects, networking and communal events, and developmental workshops.

name: Texas Energy Consulting
summary: Student run energy consulting group that offers members opportunities to gain practical knowledge in the industry through pro-bono projects, case competitions, mentorship programs, and more.

name: Lifelong Learning with Friends at the University of Texas at Austin
summary: Lifelong Learning with Friends is a 501(c)3 organization that provides inclusive educational and social programs to both UT Austin students and adults with intellectual and developmental disabilities.

name: Run for St. Jude
summary: RFSJ is a student-led run club that also fundraises for the St. Jude Austin Walk. Our group runs are 3 miles and take place on Monday, Wednesday, and Thursday evenings. No running experience is needed, and beginners are strongly encouraged to join.

name: Health and Society Collective
summary: Health and Society Collective aims to build community among Health and Society majors and welcomes all students interested in the social, cultural, and policy aspects of health through opportunities for leadership, service, and professional development.

name: The Global Business and Economics Club
summary: The Global Business and Economics Club seeks to enable its members to help each other better understand global business and economic issues, build their global network and explore career opportunities together.

name: Texas Science Bowl
summary: The Texas Science Bowl hosts an annual invitational competition in the style of the National Science Bowl. The organizational mission is to foster interest in science through fast-paced, buzzer-style competition.

name: Longhorn Developers
summary: Home of the UT Registration Plus Chrome Extension! Longhorn Developers (LHD) is a community of passionate students at UT Austin dedicated to building innovative solutions that address challenges faced by students.

name: Hooked Media Strategy
summary: HMS fosters collaboration and creativity to inspire community impact. We enhance clients’ online media presence to amplify missions, connect with audiences, and support Austin nonprofits and small businesses through full-service social media services.

name: Catalyst Consulting & Strategy Group
summary: Catalyst Consulting and Strategy Group (CCSG) seeks to bridge the gap between academic knowledge and professional practice by offering consulting and fostering the growth of future business leaders.

name: Lambda Pi Eta UT Chapter
summary: LPH's goal is to promote academic excellence in communication studies, foster interest in the communication field, facilitate discussions and exchange of ideas, and build strong relationships between faculty and students.

name: Texas Retail & E-Commerce Ventures
summary: Students interested in e-commerce and digital entrepreneurship have a place to collaborate, learn, and grow together. From building online stores on platforms like Shopify to mastering digital marketing. Texas REV has you covered.

name: Texas Space Society
summary: The Texas Space Society (TSS) is designed to promote National Space Society competitions. Through industry-level competitions, members of TSS will engage in complex, futuristic design challenges that closely mimic the dynamics of the aerospace teams.

name: Longhorn Asset Management
summary: The Premier Student-Led Hedge Fund Simulation at UT Austin

name: First-Gen Longhorns
summary: First-Gen Longhorns is committed to supporting and celebrating first-generation college students at UT Austin.

name: IDSA at UT Austin
summary: Industrial Designers Society of America student chapter.

name: Survivor Texas
summary: Survivor Texas is a student-led social strategy competition where players outwit, outplay, & outlast each other through challenges, alliances, and blindsides in the ultimate test of adaptability and resilience. Compete, join crew, or enjoy social events!

name: Investigative Reporters and Editors at UT
summary: Investigative Reporters and Editors at UT (IRE) is an organization that hopes to increase interest in investigative journalism through connecting students with professionals and other passionate students. IRE at UT is open to all!

name: Lawtina Network
summary: Lawtina is a pre-law organization originally founded at St. Mary’s University in San Antonio, TX. It is beginning to make its way through other schools in chapters and has now reached the University of Texas at Austin. It is designed to empower, support,

name: University Accounting Organization
summary: The University Accounting Organization seeks to create an inclusive, collaborative community for undergraduate accounting students at The University of Texas at Austin.

name: National Council of Negro Women, UT Austin Section
summary: It is a part of NCNW’s mission to lead, advocate for, and empower women of African descent, their families, and communities. It was founded in 1935 by Dr. Mary McLeod Bethune, an influential educator and activist.

name: Honors Consulting Association
summary: We connect UT Austin students with local businesses and organizations, delivering innovative solutions to real-world challenges. We empower students to apply academic knowledge beyond the classroom while strengthening community ties.

name: Vibe Coding Club
summary: Texas Vibe Coding is for everyone—beginners to advanced coders—who want to learn, collaborate, and build real projects that can change the world. Join us for workshops, app-building, and a community that turns ideas into impact.

name: Texas Future Lawyers
summary: Texas Future Lawyers is a UT Austin student organization that prepares undergrads for law school and beyond. We focus on academics through weekly LSAT workshops, career panels, and professional development, while also giving back through philanthropy.

name: Unwiring Minds
summary: Unwiring Minds explores the mind through hands-on education, research, and service. We connect students with real-world neuroscience, facilitate debates with leading experts, and support community-centered learning and outreach across Austin.

name: The Cyber Directive
summary: The Cyber Directive is a student-led, interdisciplinary academic journal that focuses on the intersection of cybersecurity law, policy, and technology. We provide students nationwide a platform to share research and collaborate with like-minded peers.

name: Texas Corporate Law Collective
summary: We provide a structured and practical Business Law curriculum designed to help students understand the legal principles that govern commercial activity.

name: Texas Sociological Association
summary: A professional student organization dedicated to creating pathways for students to apply sociology in their careers through academic exploration, professional development, and research opportunities.

name: Texas Voices of Equity
summary: Texas Voices of Equity is a service and community awareness-based organization dedicated to educating and empowering students to provide actionable steps to improve health equity among diverse populations.

name: Longhorn Banking Group
summary: We help train the next generation of longhorns for WSFM and IB recruitment.

name: Texas Research Association
summary: Texas Research Association is a student-run organization which facilitates research through pathways such as AI and Biology. We focus on research to foster technical expertise and produce publishable, impactful work.

name: Feminist Policy Alliance Austin
summary: The Feminist Policy Alliance is an inclusive community for students focusing on policy issues affecting women. FPA brings education and advocacy to the LBJ School, the University and the Austin community.

name: Ascend Consulting Group
summary: The UT Austin chapter of Ascend Consulting Group is a student-run consulting organization that partners with tech startups to provide real-world solutions.

name: Euphony at Texas
summary: Euphony is dedicated to fostering a collaborative, creative, and welcoming community around programming, artificial intelligence, and emerging technologies.

name: NOIRE
summary: Empowering Black creative visionaries the University of Texas at Austin.

name: Grain Photo Club
summary: Grain Photo Club is a space for UT students of all majors who share an interest in photography. We focus on building community, sharing perspectives, and finding inspiration together. Whether through conversation, collaboration, or showcasing our work.

name: Responsible Intelligence Network
summary: Our mission is to empower college students to develop and apply AI responsibly. RIN creates a space to explore AI technologiesOur mission is to empower college students to develop and apply AI responsibly. RIN creates a space to explore AI technologies,

name: Latin American Aspiring Law Student Association
summary: LAALSA is dedicated to advancing advocacy, empowerment, and educational access for Latino undergraduate students with aspirations in the legal field. We strive to cultivate a supportive and inclusive community that uplifts Latino voices.

name: Fynix
summary: Social organization that prioritizes uplifting local small businesses and the community among UT students, as well as donating/volunteering at local charities, while also promoting Austin culture and fun

name: American Adages Society
summary: A student-led society at the University of Texas exploring the wisdom of adages through discussion, research, and creativity, reviving timeless sayings as a bridge between language, culture, and modern thought.

name: KSEA Chapter at the University of Texas
summary:
Promote the application of science and technology for the general welfare of society
Help Scientists and Engineers develop their full career potential
Serve the Korean-American Communities

name: Children's Advocacy Initiative
summary: CAI is a student-led organization focused on advocacy, education, outreach, and research related to child abuse prevention and child welfare.

name: Texas Midshipmen
summary: Texas Midshipmen is the student organization representing the Naval Reserve Officers’ Training Corps. Our purpose is to support the development of midshipmen, promote leadership and professionalism, and engage the campus community.

name: Redeemed Campus Fellowship
summary: The organization aims to help its members grow spiritually. It also provides career guide and mentorship.

name: Hispanic Latino Student Association
summary: The Hispanic Latino Student Association is to provide a space where all students can celebrate and appreciate culture while also fostering a strong community among students, by hosting volunteering, social, and academic events.

summary: The Consciousness Studies Circle provides a platform for academic discussion and philosophical inquiry into consciousness, nonduality, and related topics in philosophy of mind, cognitive science, and AI.

summary: Spotlights is a program dedicated to helping establish fellowship between students in the UTeach Theatre program.

summary: The Diplomatic Table creates an inclusive, respectful space for students at The University of Texas at Austin to discuss a variety of policy topics mediated by foreign service officers (US diplomats).

name: Nuclear Is Clean Energy
summary: Nuclear is Clean Energy (NiCE) is dedicated to educating the public about nuclear energy by engaging students across all disciplines to promote its safe, efficient, and innovative role in the clean-energy sector. For more information, join our Slack.

name: AMS at UT Austin
summary: The AMS Student Chapter at UT Austin will build a welcoming community for students interested in atmospheric and related sciences. Our goal is to foster connection, collaboration, and professional growth while serving as a hub for learning & networking.

name: Engineers in Business
summary: Engineers in Business is a student organization dedicated to equipping engineering students with ample resources in order to ensure their success in the cut throat business world.

name: Billable Laughs Stand-Up Comedy
summary: Billable Laughs is UT Law’s stand-up comedy group. We meet biweekly to write + workshop jokes, hit open mics, attend comedy shows, and host an annual law school showcase. Open to students, alumni, and staff.

name: Kappa Alpha Pi Beta Sigma Chapter
summary: Kappa Alpha Pi is a pre law and government professional fraternity that focuses on fostering brotherhood and upholding community, service, and academic/professionalsuccess.

name: Viridis Fund
summary: The Viridis Fund aims to teach students fundamentals of holistic investment management through thematic and sector-based investments in a student-managed portfolio.

name: Hispanic Undergraduates in Geological Sciences UT
summary: A professional, social, and academic organization catered to showcasing Hispanic student cultural and community excellence with a diverse and open approach to community building.

name: 360 Strategy Texas
summary: We are a pioneering consulting student organization focused on delivering projects to clients through a unique framework of understanding the root of the issue first: Design Thinking.

name: Civic Leadership Council
summary: The Civic Leadership Council serves as the legislative voice of the School of Civic Leadership, representing student opinions and facilitating a strong relation between, the school, faculty, students, and university community.

name: Behavioral and Social Data Science Society
summary: We’re a professional and social student org at UT Austin for anyone interested in Behavioral & Social Data Science. We connect students with peers, faculty, and alumni, explore careers, host networking events, and offer advising. All majors welcome!

name: The Veneka Initiative
summary: The Veneka Initiative is a fundraising and advocacy organization dedicated to supporting Venezuelans. The organization’s purpose is to raise funds and awareness by selling Venezuelan arepas and donating proceeds to the Saludos Connection nonprofit.
